item.title: backlog add --ac-verify missing validate_arg allows empty verify command
item.intent: backlog add must reject --ac-verify without a value — an empty verify command silently creates a broken AC entry that fails at sprint verification time with no clear error.
item.description: In cmd_backlog_add(), all value-bearing flags call validate_arg except --ac-verify (lines 3652-3659). The companion cmd_backlog_edit() correctly calls validate_arg for --ac-verify at line 3915. Without the guard, 'backlog add --ac "check it" --ac-verify' (no value) either causes an unbound variable crash under set -u or silently creates {"text":"check it","verify":""} with an empty verify command. At sprint time, the empty verify command fails with a confusing error rather than at edit time where the fix is obvious.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_backlog_add() and locate the --ac-verify case (around line 3652)
Add validate_arg "$1" "${2:-}" || return 1 before the if [[ -z "$pending_ac" ]] check, matching line 3915 in cmd_backlog_edit()
Verify: 'backlog add --ac "check" --ac-verify' (no value) prints error and exits non-zero
Verify: 'backlog add --ac "check" --ac-verify "make test"' still creates the correct AC entry
acceptanceCriteria: backlog add --ac 'text' --ac-verify (no value) exits non-zero and prints an error
validate_arg is called for --ac-verify consistent with cmd_backlog_edit
